items.title: Syrian rebel forces seize Damascus as Assad regime topples
items.author: Channel 4 News
items.content:
The capture of the Syrian capital Damascus was unprecedented -  with rebel forces apparently meeting little or no resistance. 
(Subscribe: https://bit.ly/C4_News_Subscribe) 

The rebel commander of the main group Hayat Tahrir al Sham - known as HTS - said in a statement read out on Syrian TV: "the future is ours and that there is no turning back". 

This as the whereabouts of the fallen Syrian President, Bashar al Assad, remain unknown, with only Russia confirming he had fled the country.
-----------------------

items.title: Inside Syria's brutal prisons
items.author: Channel 4 News
items.content:
Thousands of Syrians including women and children have been freed from the country's notorious underground prisons as rebels take over the country.

[Subscribe: https://bit.ly/C4_News_Subscribe]

Damascus's infamous Sednaya prison has previously been described as a 'human slaughterhouse' by Amnesty International due to the level of torture and executions under the Assad regime.

-------

items.title: Will UK lift ban on Syria's HTS rebel group?
items.author: Channel 4 News
items.content:
Syria is now mostly under the control of the rebel group Hayat Tahrir al Sham. 

[Subscribe: https://bit.ly/C4_News_Subscribe]

Their leader Abu Mohammed al-Jolani has pledged to have a representative government and promote religious tolerance - although some are sceptical.
The UK now needs to decide whether to lift the ban on HTS, which they consider a terrorist organisation.

items.title: Jay-Z accused of raping13-year-old
items.author: Channel 4 News
items.content:
Jay-Z has been accused in a civil lawsuit of raping a 13-year-old girl in 2000. 

[Subscribe: https://bit.ly/C4_News_Subscribe]

The rapper, whose real name is Shawn Carter, is accused along with Sean 'Diddy' Combs, of drugging and assaulting the girl at a party in New York. Both men deny the allegations and Jay Z described the lawsuit as a "blackmail attempt".

items.title: Assad falls: should it be a moment of joy or fear for Syria?
items.author: Channel 4 News
items.content:
Bashar al-Assad has fled Damascus to Russia where Vladimir Putin is reportedly sheltering the former Syrian dictator - for those Syrians who have defied Assad’s brutal regime for years this is a moment of celebration and hope, but also fear.

[Subscribe: https://bit.ly/C4_News_Subscribe]

Will Syria manage to move on from more than a decade of war to become a democratic state, or will it descend into further chaos and violence like Libya and Iraq did after liberation from their dictators?

On this week’s episode of the Political Fourcast, Krishnan Guru-Murthy is joined by Syrian filmmaker and journalist Waad Al-Kateab, whose documentary For Sama won numerous awards and was nominated for an Oscar, and Mazen Gharibah who is director of the Syrian British Consortium.

Produced by Calum Fraser, Ka Yee Mak, Silvia Maresca
-------

items.title: Chancellor meets EU for first time after Brexit to ‘reset’ relations
items.author: Channel 4 News
items.content:
The return of the prodigal child or merely a gentle attempt to offer an olive branch?

(Subscribe: https://bit.ly/C4_News_Subscribe) 

For the first time since Brexit, a UK chancellor was back in Brussels addressing her European Union counterparts.

Rachel Reeves is calling her mission a 'reset' which is necessary, she says, to restore good trading relationships with our nearest neighbours and a boost to the economy.

items.title: Syria: Inside Sednaya prison
items.author: Channel 4 News
items.content:
After the fast moving events of the past week when rebels took major cities and gained control of Syria, today the extent of the brutality of the Assad regime was revealed by the liberation of a notorious prison in the capital, Damascus.

[Subscribe: https://bit.ly/C4_News_Subscribe]

Foreign Affairs correspondent Pariac O'Brien reached Damascus after travelling by road from the Lebanese border.

Along the route he and the team met a unit of the HTS, the Islamist rebel group which now controls Syria.

Camera and edit: Jack Hewson

items.content:

In this episode of Ways to Change the World, Djalili says a combination of his Baha’i faith, his heritage and the encouragement of friends is what has kept him resilient in the face of setbacks, including being “cancelled” after the 9/11 attacks.

items.title: CEO shooting suspect Luigi Mangione charged with murder
items.author: Channel 4 News
items.content:
It was a private funeral after the most public of deaths - the family of Brian Thompson, the CEO of one of America’s biggest health insurers, said their farewells in Minnesota yesterday, with armed officers stationed outside.

(Subscribe: https://bit.ly/C4_News_Subscribe) 

After a week in which his death became a lightning rod for many Americans’ frustration with their healthcare - and his suspected killer became a cult hero for some on the internet - a 26-year-old man is now in custody and charged with murder.
